faqs.a: It's a DSM-5-TR personality disorder in the schizophrenia spectrum group, marked by deep social discomfort, odd beliefs or perceptions, and eccentric behavior. The unusual experiences stop short of full psychosis.

faqs.q: Is schizotypal personality disorder the same as schizophrenia?
faqs.a: No, though they're related. The odd beliefs and perceptions in schizotypal personality disorder usually don't reach the level of fixed delusions or clear hallucinations, and the person generally keeps more of a grip on shared reality.

faqs.q: How is it different from schizoid personality disorder?
faqs.a: Schizoid personality disorder centers on genuine indifference to relationships. Schizotypal personality disorder includes odd thinking and perception plus intense social anxiety driven by suspicion.
